topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink on the paper seemed to vibrate under my gaze. I picked up the graded essay, my fingers trembling slightly against the crisp white page. A B-minus. It was a significant drop from the A I had been aiming for. My stomach performed a slow, unpleasant flip. I reread the feedback, the professor’s critiques sharp and precise, each sentence landing like a tiny stone in my chest. I kept running my thumb over the corner of the paper, a repetitive motion that offered no comfort. When the professor finally called my name, the sound echoed in the quiet lecture hall, and I felt a sudden, intense heat rush up my neck. I managed a shaky nod, the effort of composure straining every muscle in my body.

[story 2]

My hands were slick as I tried to open the envelope. The paper felt unusually heavy in my grasp. When I finally unfolded the report, the numerical score hit me like a physical blow. It was lower than I had calculated, a disappointing reflection of weeks of late nights fueled by caffeine and sheer panic. I quickly scanned the comments, my eyes darting back and forth across the dense text. A tightness constricted my throat, making it difficult to draw a full breath. I leaned back in my chair, trying to appear relaxed, but my shoulders were hiked up near my ears. I wanted to disappear, to somehow dissolve into the worn upholstery of the seat, wishing the floor would simply swallow me whole.

[story 3]

The fluorescent lights of the library hummed, amplifying the silence surrounding my desk. I stared at the transcript, my breath catching in shallow gasps. The grade was undeniably lower than the A I had envisioned. A cold trickle began at my fingertips and spread rapidly up my arms. I gripped my pen so tightly that my knuckles turned white. Every small sound—the rustle of a passing page, the distant tap of a keyboard—seemed magnified, demanding attention. I slowly pushed the paper away, the movement stiff and deliberate, as if moving through thick water. I closed my eyes for a brief second, trying to gather the scattered pieces of my focus before forcing myself to confront the disappointing reality laid out before me.
